questions.question: Consider a university management system where `Person` is the base class with attributes name and email, `Student` inherits from Person with additional attributes student_id and course, and `Research Scholar` inherits from Student with attributes research_area and supervisor. Further, `Teaching Assistant` inherits from both Person and a class named `Employee` (with attributes employee_id and salary). (a) Draw the inheritance hierarchy diagram showing all relationships. (b) Write the complete Python code implementing this scenario with appropriate __init__ methods using super() for code reusability. (c) Explain the Method Resolution Order (MRO) when creating a Teaching Assistant object. (d) Demonstrate how super() resolves the constructor chain in this multilevel and multiple inheritance scenario.
keyPoints: Identify and represent multilevel inheritance (Person -> Student -> Research Scholar)
Identify and represent multiple inheritance (Teaching Assistant inherits from Person and Employee)
Implement proper __init__ methods using super() to avoid code duplication
Use MRO to trace the order of method resolution using ClassName.mro() or __mro__
Explain how super() follows MRO to call parent class methods in the correct order

questions.question: Design a shape hierarchy for a graphics application using abstract base classes. Create an abstract class `Shape` with abstract methods area() and perimeter(), then implement three concrete classes: `Circle` (with radius), `Rectangle` (with length and width), and `Triangle` (with three sides). (a) Write the complete Python code using the abc module. (b) Create a function calculate_total_area that accepts a list of Shape objects and returns the sum of their areas. (c) Demonstrate polymorphism by iterating over a list containing objects of different shape types and calling their area() method. (d) Explain why we cannot instantiate the Shape class directly and how abstract methods enforce implementation in derived classes.
keyPoints: Use ABC class and @abstractmethod decorator from abc module
Implement concrete classes with complete area() and perimeter() methods
Demonstrate runtime polymorphism through method overriding
Show type-agnostic processing using a list of base class references
Explain the purpose and enforcement mechanism of abstract methods

questions.question: In a hospital management system, there is a base class `Doctor` with method treat_patient(), and two derived classes `Surgeon` (with method perform_surgery()) and `Cardiologist` (with method perform_checkup()). A `MedicalTeam` class inherits from both Surgeon and Cardiologist. (a) Write Python code demonstrating this hybrid inheritance structure. (b) If MedicalTeam has a method treat_patient() that calls both perform_surgery() and perform_checkup(), explain how the treat_patient() method resolution works. (c) Create a MedicalTeam object and demonstrate calling all three methods (treat_patient, perform_surgery, perform_checkup).
keyPoints: Implement hybrid inheritance combining hierarchical and multiple inheritance
Define base class Doctor with treat_patient() method
Create derived classes Surgeon and Cardiologist inheriting from Doctor
Implement MedicalTeam inheriting from both Surgeon and Cardiologist
Demonstrate method invocation and resolution in hybrid inheritance

questions.question: You are developing a vehicle rental system where `Vehicle` is the base class, `Car` and `Motorcycle` inherit from Vehicle, and `ElectricCar` inherits from Car. (a) Write complete Python classes demonstrating this hierarchical inheritance. (b) Create a function check_rental_eligibility(vehicle) that uses isinstance() to determine if the vehicle can be rented based on its type (e.g., only ElectricCar vehicles with battery > 50% are eligible for premium rental). (c) Write another function verify_vehicle_hierarchy(obj) that uses issubclass() to check if a given object belongs to a specific class hierarchy. (d) Create instances of each class and demonstrate both functions with appropriate test cases showing the output.
keyPoints: Implement hierarchical inheritance (Vehicle -> Car -> ElectricCar and Vehicle -> Motorcycle)
Use isinstance() to check object types at runtime for conditional logic
Use issubclass() to check class inheritance relationships
Combine isinstance() with specific attribute conditions for business logic
Demonstrate practical application of type checking in real-world scenarios

questions.question: Consider a class hierarchy for an online learning platform: `User` (base), `Student` inherits from User, `Instructor` inherits from User, and `TeachingAssistant` inherits from both Student and Instructor. (a) Write Python code with proper constructor overriding using super() such that each class's __init__ prints a message identifying which class is being initialized. (b) Create a TeachingAssistant object and trace the exact order in which all __init__ methods are called. (c) If you remove the super() call from one of the intermediate classes, explain what would happen and why. (d) Modify the code to pass specific arguments to initialize attributes like name, email, student_id, instructor_id, and department for TeachingAssistant.
keyPoints: Demonstrate constructor chaining through multiple levels of inheritance
Show how super() follows MRO to call parent constructors in order
Illustrate the consequences of omitting super() calls
Implement proper attribute initialization in each class
Trace and explain the complete initialization sequence
